question: In a clinical trial in which a total of 100 patients are allocated to two treatments by simple randomization, show that the probability that the difference between the numbers of patients in the two treatment groups exceeds 20 is about $5 \%$. (Hint: consider the distribution of the number of patients allocated to one of the groups.)
answer: Another way of phrasing the question is, 'What is the probability of the number in one group being less than 40 or more than $60 ?$
